name: Ingest Module
summary: Successfully built the ingest module for meeting-system-v3 that normalizes various transcript formats and creates v3 manifests with microsummaries

tasks.notes: Built comprehensive ingest module with support for .md, .txt, .docx, .jsonl formats
All formats handled with appropriate parsing. JSONL converts to 'speaker: text' format as specified

tasks.task: Convert jsonl to markdown (speaker: text format)
tasks.notes: JSONL files are parsed and converted to speaker: text format with robust error handling for malformed JSON
Integrated /zo/ask API to generate one-paragraph microsummaries with proper error handling and fallbacks
V3 manifests created following the schema with status='ingested' and proper timestamps. Includes metadata extraction from filenames
Full CLI interface with help, error handling, dry-run mode, single file and folder processing

acceptance_criteria.microsummary_generated: ✅ LLM-generated microsummary via /zo/ask with error handling
acceptance_criteria.manifest_created_correctly: ✅ V3 manifest follows schema, passes validation, includes all required fields
acceptance_criteria.dry_run_mode_works: ✅ --dry-run shows what would be done without creating files

key_features: Comprehensive format support including JSONL with multiple schema patterns
Intelligent date extraction from filenames with multiple formats
Meeting ID generation following YYYY-MM-DD_Meeting-Name convention
Duration estimation based on transcript word count
Title extraction and normalization from filenames
Microsummary generation with 8000-char truncation for token limits
Schema-compliant v3 manifest with proper status transitions
Robust error handling for short transcripts, malformed files, API failures
validation_performed: Schema validation passes for generated manifests
Folder-level processing tested with multiple files
Error conditions tested (short files, missing files, invalid formats)
JSONL parsing tested with speaker: text conversion
dependencies_integrated: Uses manifest-v3.schema.json from D2.1 for schema compliance
Follows meeting ID conventions for downstream processing

test_cases.result: ✅ Success - converted to speaker: text format
✅ Success - proper error message for short transcript
✅ Success - shows actions without creating files

dependencies_satisfied: D2.1 - Uses manifest-v3.schema.json for schema compliance and validation
next_steps: Module ready for integration with identify module (D3.2) and quality gate (D3.4) in the processing pipeline
